title: Montage
explanation: A montage joins short shots so time and change are felt rather than watched in full. Editing for rhythm and contrast lets meaning emerge from the cut itself.
usedInGameplay: Solves a slow second act and raises pacing and structure.

title: Close-up
explanation: A close-up isolates a face or detail and gives it emotional weight. The audience reads the smallest reaction, so the moment lands without dialogue.
usedInGameplay: Solves a flat character beat and raises emotion and performance.

title: Long take
explanation: A long take holds a single unbroken shot, building tension and realism in real time. It demands precise planning because there is no cut to hide behind.
usedInGameplay: Raises visual style and emotion when a tense scene must not be broken.

title: Jump cut
explanation: A jump cut breaks continuity inside a shot, skipping time and creating a restless, modern energy that draws attention to the edit.
usedInGameplay: Raises pacing and visual style when a passage feels static.

title: Voiceover
explanation: Voiceover lays narration over the image to reach a character's inner life. It is efficient, but it can weaken visual storytelling if it explains too much.
usedInGameplay: Raises emotion and structure, with a risk to visual style.

title: Natural light
explanation: Shooting with available light gives realism and a lower cost, trading total control for honesty. It rewards planning around the time of day.
usedInGameplay: Raises visual style and quality when the budget is tight.

title: Cross-cutting
explanation: Cross-cutting alternates between two parallel actions so the audience feels them converge. It is a core engine of suspense and momentum.
usedInGameplay: Raises pacing and audience appeal when two threads need tension.

title: Match cut
explanation: A match cut links two shots by shape or movement so a transition reads as a thematic connection rather than a break.
usedInGameplay: Raises visual style and structure when a transition feels abrupt.

title: Handheld camera
explanation: A handheld camera adds immediacy and physical presence, putting the audience inside the moment at the cost of a polished image.
usedInGameplay: Raises visual style and emotion when a scene feels too clean.

title: Offscreen sound
explanation: Sound from outside the frame expands the world and implies threat or scale the camera never shows, doing more with less.
usedInGameplay: Raises sound and emotion when a scene needs danger without spectacle.
